paper_title: Cartographic and OSL Dating Methods in Araranguá River Basin Study

methods.description: This method involves the use of cartographic tools to identify fluvial, fluvial-marine, and marine terraces in the Araranguá River basin, followed by field sampling for physical analyses and dating.

steps.description: The Araranguá River basin (SC) delimitation, Digital Elevation Model (DEM) development, topographic profiles construction, and location of sampling points were all performed by the ArcGIS software 10.5.1 and Global Mapper 18.0.
steps.searchable_summary: Use ArcGIS and Global Mapper to develop DEM and locate sampling points

steps.description: Radar images with 30-meter spatial resolution were used as the cartographic base.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch radar images with 30-meter resolution for cartographic base

steps.description: Trenches were opened in the levels of the terraces. Later, samples of the covering materials of the marine geomorphological surfaces were collected and identified for physical analyses and OSL absolute dating in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Open trenches and collect samples for physical analysis and OSL dating

methods.description: This method uses OSL dating to determine the age of sediment samples by measuring the luminescence signal related to trapped electrons in crystalline solids.

steps.description: The method developed by Huntley et al. (1985) enabled measuring the luminescence signal directly related to the load of an electron population trapped in crystalline solids (minerals), found in the very deposits through light stimulus.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure luminescence signal in minerals using light stimulus

steps.description: Age (I) is obtained by measuring the dose stored in the grains analyzed (P), divided by the ionizing radiation rate.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate age by dividing paleodose by ionizing radiation rate

steps.description: The annual doses and radiation rates of sediments were obtained from determination of the levels of U 235 / U 238 (Uranium), Th 232 (ppm) (Thorium), and K 40 (%) (Potassium), by γ-ray spectroscopy of the natural sample without any treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine annual doses and radiation rates using γ-ray spectroscopy

steps.description: Absolute dating by Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) were performed by the DATAÇÃO Laboratory using the SAR (Single Aliquot Regenerative-dose) in quartz grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform OSL dating using SAR method on quartz grains

steps.description: The sample collections of surface materials from the terraces for OSL absolute dating were carried out through the opening of trenches. For this purpose, Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) tubes with dark colors were used, of approximately 60 centimeters of length and six centimeters of diameter, so the samples were not exposed to solar radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples using PVC tubes to prevent solar exposure
